mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market exceeding a specific unemployment threshold signals peak economic activity and impending earnings contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns turn negative when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated while consumer price inflation accelerates, real borrowing costs stay restrictive, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite high real rates and rising inflation, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a 5-day forward return regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with expanding corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decouples from S&P 500 earnings revisions for two consecutive years, or forward returns show no correlation with GDP growth surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in nonfarm payrolls below a specific monthly threshold indicates weakening aggregate demand, which forces downward earnings revisions and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Market rallies despite payroll misses due to dominant monetary easing, or the threshold breach shows no incremental predictive power over baseline unemployment data.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling residential construction signals sectoral rebalancing toward commercial activity, which boosts industrial and financial equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts negative returns when both series move in opposite directions, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, sustained growth in real disposable income fuels consumer spending resilience, supporting corporate cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Consumer spending growth fails to translate into equity outperformance when rates exceed 0.5%, or the signal produces negative forward returns in high-rate quarters.
